tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 - Property Acquisition
tool_args.raw_text: Approved the acquisition from the City of New York, Department of Housing Preservation and Development, pursuant to Public Authorities Law Section 1266-c, of property located at 1807-09 and 1811-13 Park Avenue (Block 1773, Lots 4 & 72), New York, N.Y., in support of Second Avenue Subway – Phase 2 project.

payload.description: Property acquisition for Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 - acquisition of 1807-09 and 1811-13 Park Avenue properties from NYC Department of Housing Preservation and Development

evidence_refs.source_quote: Approved the acquisition from the City of New York, Department of Housing Preservation and Development, pursuant to Public Authorities Law Section 1266-c, of property located at 1807-09 and 1811-13 Park Avenue (Block 1773, Lots 4 & 72), New York, N.Y., in support of Second Avenue Subway – Phase 2 project

tool_args.label: Procurement of up to 1,729 Buses
tool_args.raw_text: Request to issue competitive Request for Proposals for the purchase of up to 1,729 buses (952 for NYC Transit and 777 for MTA Bus Company) through multiple solicitations.

payload.description: Procurement of up to 1,729 buses (952 for NYC Transit and 777 for MTA Bus Company) through multiple competitive RFPs, including 1,042 low-floor 40-foot buses, 345 low-floor 60-foot buses, and 342 over-the-road express buses; 485 of the low-floor buses will be all-electric

evidence_refs.source_quote: Purchase of up to 1,729 buses (952 for NYC Transit and 777 for MTA Bus Company) through multiple solicitations

The remaining 1,729 buses include 1,042 low-floor 40-foot buses, 345 low-floor 60-foot buses, and 342 over-the-road express buses; 485 of the low-floor buses will be all-electric (460 for NYC Transit and 25 for MTABC)

tool_args.label: 347 Madison Avenue Development
tool_args.raw_text: 347 MADISON AVE DEVELOPMENT - MTA Board resolution for long-term lease of property interests

payload.description: 99-year triple net lease with Boston Properties for redevelopment of MTA's former headquarters site at 341-347 Madison Avenue into a commercial office building with transit improvements

evidence_refs.source_quote: AGENCY: Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") DEVELOPER: BP 347 Madison Associates, LLC ... owned by Boston Properties Limited Partnership
